title: Mass. man sentenced for illegally trafficking dozens of endangered animal parts
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adam Bied, 40, pleaded guilty to wildlife trafficking, smuggling more than 100 endangered animal parts from 2018 to 2021.
Bied received an eight-month prison sentence, two years of supervised release, and a $75,000 fine for wildlife violations.
He imported endangered species parts from Cameroon and Indonesia, including tiger skulls and narwhal tusks, disguising them as 'decorative masks.'

title: Taunton man convicted in fatal stabbing of Falmouth 19-year-old
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A Taunton man, Adrian Black, was convicted of first-degree murder for fatally stabbing Milteer Hendricks in 2023.
Black and four others drove to Falmouth intending to attack Hendricks, with Black delivering the fatal chest stab.
Hendricks died after being med-flighted to Boston; Black was arrested two days later and faces life imprisonment.

title: State Police lieutenant named in report about sergeant’s alleged drunk driving reportedly suspended
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lt. Anthony DeLucia suspended with pay amid investigation into Sgt. Scott Quigley's alleged drunk driving crash.
Quigley indicted for negligent motor vehicle homicide; alleged crash victim Angelo Schettino died in January 2024.
DeLucia allegedly advised not to disclose Quigley's blood alcohol level after crash, inquiry reveals.

title: Mass. couple sentenced to prison for manslaughter in death of infant foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adams couple sentenced to three to five years for manslaughter after 10-month-old foster child died in 2020.
Child died due to untreated strep infection and bronchopneumonia; couple ignored signs needing medical care.
Sentences staggered for couple who have four other children; both banned from future caregiving roles.

title: Salisbury woman gets life without parole for poisoning boyfriend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Judy Church, 67, from Salisbury was sentenced to life without parole for fatally poisoning Leroy Fowler in 2022.
Church was found guilty of first-degree murder after poisoning Fowler's drink with antifreeze, leading to his death.
Fowler's toxic relationship with Church included her creating a voodoo doll of another woman he was involved with.

title: 5 concerning trends from the Red Sox’ ugly start to 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Red Sox start the 2026 season poorly with a 1-5 record after being swept by the Astros.
Red Sox pitching struggles with a team ERA of 4.91 and ranks last in home runs allowed per nine innings.
Key players like Roman Anthony and Trevor Story are underperforming, impacting Boston's offensive efforts.

title: Former Patriots CB Stephon Gilmore retires from NFL
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephon Gilmore announced his retirement from the NFL at age 35 after a 13-year career.
Gilmore played for six NFL teams, including the Patriots, with whom he won Super Bowl LIII in 2018.
He earned five Pro Bowl selections, a Defensive Player of the Year award, and two first-team All-Pro titles.

title: Oracle layoffs begin globally; impact on Mass. workforce unclear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Oracle layoffs started globally, impacting multiple countries, but specific details for Massachusetts remain unclear as of April 2026.
Analysts estimate Oracle could cut 20,000-30,000 jobs, nearly 18% of its 162,000-strong global workforce.
No Oracle layoffs reported under Massachusetts’ WARN Act; Burlington and Nashua offices remain unaffected so far.

title: Ex-Patriots captain Kyle Dugger reportedly finds new AFC home
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kyle Dugger signed a one-year deal with the Cincinnati Bengals, his third AFC team in three years.
Dugger previously played with the Steelers, recording 42 tackles, two interceptions, and a sack in nine games last season.
The former Patriots captain had a standout 2023 season with 109 tackles and seven pass breakups before his numbers declined.

title: Mass. family flies home from vacation to find truck stolen from Logan Airport garage
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Brendon Byrnes returned from vacation to find his truck stolen from Logan Airport's central parking garage.
The theft shared similarities with a previous incident at the same garage weeks earlier, State Police reported.
No arrests have been made as State Police continue investigating both theft cases linked to New Jersey routes.
